label: Squarish 2BR / 1 bath — master front-left, standard rear-left, bath block + kitchen across the rear, open-plan great room takes the front-right.

adjacencies.note: master and standard stack vertically along the left wall — master at the front (street side), standard at the rear. Shared wall only, no door between them.
master bedroom door faces the great_room (master's east wall faces great's west wall)
standard's east wall overlaps great_room's west wall — hosts standard's door to the great_room
common is just east of standard at the rear; shared wall only, no door (single_bath means bath access through great_room only)
common's south wall faces great_room — door faces great for shared access by both bedrooms
shared plumbing wall — kitchen and common bath stack rear-right; bath west of kitchen
great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style)

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_2br_kitchen_shape
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Floor kitchen width at 2.0 m on compact envelopes so the work triangle stays usable.

notes: v2 catalog topology #1 — smallest squarish 2BR. Covers the 45-65 m² floor band:
Pag-IBIG-style starter homes, compact mid-market.
Program: 2BR sharing ONE common bath. No ensuite. Both bedrooms reach the bath
through the great_room (per the locked rule that no bedroom may be accessed
through a bathroom, and the inverse: bedrooms don't access baths through bedrooms).
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_preference='right'):
Front: [master    | great_room                     ]
Rear:  [standard  | common | kitchen               ]
Master sits at the front-LEFT corner; standard stacks directly behind it at
the rear-LEFT. Standard extends EAST of master by ~1 m so its south wall
overlaps the great_room's north wall — that overlap hosts standard's door to
the great room (the only way to reach the common bath without going through
the master bedroom or the bath itself).
The bath block sits BETWEEN standard and kitchen (standard | common | kitchen)
at the rear. Kitchen anchors to the rear-right corner (right_anchored + the
solver's hard kitchen-rear rule). The great_room spans the front-right area,
hosting the main entry on its front (south) wall.
Carport: 3.0 m wide x 6.0 m deep (Item 2 default single carport). With 2 m
side and front setbacks the carport's 1 m width + 4 m depth that exceeds the
setbacks is reclaimed by a building_void (1 x 4 m, front-right corner). The
result is an L-shaped building footprint with the carport sitting flush
against the lot's front-right corner. The void eats 4 sqm out of the
great_room's front-right corner.
When the brief sets carport_preference='left', the runner mirrors the topology
so master ends up front-right, standard rear-right, and kitchen rear-left.
